authoritative_sources.title: FSMA Final Rule for Preventive Controls for Human Food
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's preventive-controls rule page is the authoritative public baseline for facilities that manufacture, process, pack, or hold human food and it defines execution-relevant monitoring, corrective-action, and supply-chain expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: line readiness, in-process control, corrective-action, and supplier-program boundary review

authoritative_sources.title: 21 CFR Part 117 - Current Good Manufacturing Practice, Hazard Analysis, and Risk-Based Preventive Controls for Human Food
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 21 CFR Part 117 is the primary public regulatory baseline for cGMP, holding, warehousing, distribution, records, and corrective actions that constrain food manufacturing execution and closure.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: food manufacturing execution, records posture, warehousing context, and finished-goods handling

authoritative_sources.title: Hazard Analysis and Risk-Based Preventive Controls for Human Food: Guidance for Industry
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's guidance for industry is the official operating reference for applying preventive-controls, records, and supply-chain program expectations to plant workflow decisions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: records review, preventive-control execution, and supplier-program handoff

authoritative_sources.title: FSMA Final Rule on Requirements for Additional Traceability Records for Certain Foods
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's traceability-rule page is the authoritative public baseline for Critical Tracking Events and Key Data Elements that shape lot-aware execution and escalation for covered foods.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: ingredient receipt, lot assignment, traceability reconciliation, and hold or release review

authoritative_sources.title: Initiation of Voluntary Recalls Under 21 CFR Part 7, Subpart C
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA recall guidance is an authoritative operating reference for when inventory, batches, or distributions must stop moving through normal execution and shift into hold, recall, or formal quality-led disposition.
used_for: refusal to continue execution under recall conditions

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 Global Traceability Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 is the recognized standards body for identifier and traceability exchange standards used across suppliers, plants, warehouses, and downstream food-manufacturing handoffs.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: capture and reconciliation of item, lot, location, and event data

authoritative_sources.title: Core Business Vocabulary (CBV) Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 CBV is the recognized public reference for business-step and event vocabulary that keeps ERP, MES, WMS, and supplier traceability evidence semantically aligned.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: cross-system event naming and handoff semantics

authoritative_sources.title: SQF Food Safety Code for Food Manufacturing
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SQFI publishes a widely recognized food-manufacturing code that is directly relevant to nonconforming product, food-safety controls, documented operating discipline, and release-boundary handling in plant execution.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: in-process control, nonconforming product handling, and release-boundary discipline

workflow_stages.name: production order intake and line readiness review
workflow_stages.description: Review production orders, batch instructions, approved materials, sanitation or allergen prerequisites, and startup conditions to determine whether work can begin.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: material issuance, batch execution, and in-process control
workflow_stages.description: Validate that the right ingredient and packaging lots are issued, monitor throughput and in-process checks, and keep preventive-control and hold boundaries explicit.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: exception handling for deviations, holds, and unsupported substitutions
workflow_stages.description: Track deviations, quality holds, supplier or substitution pressure, and rework or nonconformance cases while preserving formal approval ownership.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

workflow_stages.name: lot, inventory, and traceability reconciliation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm that batch, lot, inventory, and supplier records reconcile across ERP, MES, WMS, QMS, and traceability systems before movement or completion is recommended.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

workflow_stages.name: completion evidence and release-boundary handling
workflow_stages.description: Close food manufacturing work only when reconciliation, signoff, and release-dependent evidence are present, and escalate holds, recalls, or supplier-quality decisions instead of absorbing those approvals.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

artifacts: production orders, batch instructions, routings, and work-center assignments
ingredient, packaging, batch, lot, inventory, and supplier records
line-clearance, sanitation, allergen-changeover, startup, and in-process control records
quality holds, nonconformance reports, release records, deviation records, and rework authorizations
traceability logs, inventory movements, and shipment or warehouse handoff records
completion checklists, yield reconciliation, lot-consumption records, and signoff artifacts
change-control documentation, supplier substitution requests, and escalation notes

allowed: classify food manufacturing work as in-scope, out-of-scope, blocked, on hold, rework-pending, or complete based on documented execution state and evidence
request or validate the minimum tenant facts, batch records, lot context, and systems-of-record evidence needed to continue safely
prepare evidence-backed status updates, completion records, and escalation packages without overstating authority
escalate: final quality release, nonconformance disposition, recall handling, or preventive-controls signoff
engineering, formulation, process-parameter, or change-control approval
supplier approval, substitution approval, or formal supplier-quality disposition
safety override, legal interpretation, or policy-override decisions
refuse: start or continue production when required sanitation, allergen, batch-record, or traceability evidence is missing and would change the outcome
mark food manufacturing work complete without reconciliation, signoff, batch, or lot evidence
bypass traceability, hold, release, sanitation, or quality controls to protect schedule or throughput

domain_failure_modes: incorrect ingredient or packaging lot is used because authoritative issuance and genealogy records were not reconciled
line readiness is assumed without sanitation, allergen-changeover, or approved batch-instruction evidence
ERP, MES, WMS, and QMS conflict on lot, hold, or inventory state for the same batch
food manufacturing work is declared complete without yield reconciliation, lot-consumption records, or required signoff evidence
quality hold, food-safety deviation, or recall trigger is treated as a local throughput problem instead of a formal release-boundary event
supplier substitutions or rework paths are treated as routine scheduling decisions instead of supplier-quality, change-control, or QA escalations
the lane drifts into final quality, engineering, supplier approval, recall, or safety authority

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Supplier or substitution decision outside lane authority

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Food Traceability List coverage by product family
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: As of April 9, 2026, the FDA traceability rule baseline is directly applicable when the tenant's product family falls on the Food Traceability List. Tenants still must specify whether the affected product is covered before the lane treats FSMA traceability obligations as controlling for that specific run.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: SQF edition applicability by certified site and audit cycle
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: SQFI has published Edition 10, but the public code page states implementation for audits begins January 1, 2027. Sites operating on April 9, 2026 may still be executing against Edition 9 certification rules, so tenant audit-cycle context is required before using SQF details as a site-specific obligation.

notes: Authoritative batch, lot, inventory, supplier, and release records are the ERP, MES, WMS, QMS, supplier-management, and traceability records designated by the tenant, constrained by FDA, eCFR, GS1, and recognized food-safety baselines where applicable.
The lane stops before formal quality release, engineering change approval, supplier approval, recall authority, and logistics release authorization even when plant throughput is under pressure.
